title: Single consistent naming scheme for API json fields.
body:
/kind feature

**Description**

Hi,
I'm not sure whether there are any technical reasons for the naming of the API fields. A single naming scheme would simplify de-/serializing fields.

As an example, the [system -> get info method](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#operation/SystemInfoLibpod) returns json with three different naming schemes:
* `host` with camelCase
* `host` contains `idMappings` which contains `gidmap` which uses snake_case
* `version` contains PascalCase

Is it possible to unify the field names?

comments.body: Probably yes and no.  It is possible, but would it break existing API.  Would it break conformance with Docker.

> Would it break conformance with Docker.

Sorry, I was only refering to the non-compat podman API.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

If you want to open a PR to make that change, I am sure we would review it.
@mheon @vrothberg What do you think?  Would this require a major version Bump if we were to clean this up?
Yep. This is Podman 4.0 at earliest.
We should also use some rendering implementation that does not require duplicating the handlers that are currently shared between compat and libpod.

Are there any guidelines for the correct naming conventions?

Are there central folders for input and output types? I found [podman/libpod/define/](/containers/podman/tree/master/libpod/define) but I'm not sure whether there are other relevant places in the code and if so, how to find them.

The [apiv2 tests](/containers/podman/tree/master/test/apiv2) probably need to be kept in line with the naming changes, is there anything else to keep in mind?

> We should also use some rendering implementation that does not require duplicating the handlers that are currently shared between compat and libpod.

I don't think I can help with that.
I believe all API structs live in `pkg/domain/entities`
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Whatever we do, older clients should not break. We can have consistency _and_ allow the server to accept the old keys such that new users aren't confused by inconsistent naming and older clients continue running.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@jwhonce do we still have this issue?

There is currently no consistent naming in the API, neither in parameters, nor in responses.
The [system->get info endpoint](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#operation/SystemInfoLibpod) example from the first post in this issue is still using multiple naming schemes.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Unless we find a way to remain backwards compatible, we are not going to fix it. The existing deployments would break.
